name: treasure-vault
summary: A sealed room housing wealth — heaped coin, locked chests, or display cases. Often trapped, nearly always lit by the glitter itself.

primary_palette.note: dragon-style hoard — a single mound in the room's heart
mercantile vault — locked strongboxes lined along walls
collector's cabinet — named objects behind glass
working treasury — loose coin still being counted
single object of great price on a pedestal

story_prompts: a dragon's hoard long abandoned — coins tarnished, a single footprint in the dust
a merchant prince's counting vault, ledgers stacked with the ingots
a temple reliquary — one object matters, the rest is offering
a thief's stash behind a false wall, disorganized and hasty
a trapped vault where the greed itself is the puzzle
anti_patterns: don't scatter small piles across every cell — treasure reads better concentrated
don't forget the vault door — the entry should feel heavy and singular
avoid mundane furniture (chairs, tables) unless this is a counting-room variant
